channel.title: Kamrul Hassan Nirzhor Profile
channel.description: Recent content on Kamrul Hassan Nirzhor Profile

item.title: The Algorithm Said Your House Is Worth What? Bias, Accountability, and the Limits of AI Property Valuation
item.description: Imagine checking an online real estate platform and discovering that an algorithm has valued your home tens of thousands of dollars below what similar properties are selling for in your neighbourhood. You know your house has a finished basement, a renovated kitchen, and a premium lot, but the computer treats it like every other house on the street. Now imagine the opposite: the algorithm overvalues the home, a company buys it based on that estimate, and later realizes it cannot resell the property without taking a loss.

item.title: When Anonymous Isn't Anonymous: Ethical Flaws in a Course Evaluation Survey
item.description:
Introduction

Course evaluation surveys can be useful tools for improving education, but only if the information is collected honestly, fairly, and responsibly. In this scenario, students complete a ten-question survey that is described as anonymous, yet they must first log in with their organizational username and password. Management then plans to use the results to decide which courses need to be redesigned.

On the surface this looks like a routine feedback process. Underneath, it raises ethical concerns involving anonymity, informed consent, transparency, privacy, fairness, and data quality. The sample question itself also creates problems that could make the results unreliable. If students do not trust the survey, the data will not reflect their real experiences.
